generatedSentence: A unique dweller in a shacky housing unit, often mocked for his unusual fauvism-inspired handmade art, sometimes borrowed tools; his face showed confusion over whataboutism from critics, lacking formal university education, he nonetheless had insights and faith in his quiet heroism, his kit included a keyring of knives he’d learn to wield, dreaming of restoration for the federations, hoping to transform the abysmal conditions as he lived simply, surrounded by sunflowers grown from wheat, adding vitality to his aged world, often kipping on the floor. An incident occurred: a twister ripped through, causing a deadlock in the city's hierarchy; the guards were unwilling to help, leaving many hurting and confused as he saw emergents of raccoons scavenging through the ruined housing, the temperature dropped, and a strange mist rose, damping any hope, he knew he had to nominate himself, overcoming his over-reliance on liberalism as a sole solution, he felt a jerk of fear, recognizing the need to conjoin his dormant skills, his brain started working, realizing his primary problem was a lack of icebreaker action. Suddenly, a cascade of jackhammers began, signaling an uprising from below, causing the ground to waver; the lieutenant, a phantom of the old aristocrat class, watched as the dweller donned a leopard-print backpack, his original plan hatching, using his magic to generate a broad, graceful arc of ice skates from the moisture, he navigated the tangled mysteries of gravity, leaving clear footprints as he moved with gradual, cinematic lighting, avoiding the lustful gaze of the monopoly figures, his brain on stimulants, he managed to juxtapose the chaos with moments of unexpected beauty. Through the artic air, he voyaged with his electrician's kit, performing upgrades and cleaning, restoring power and light; the drums began to beat, a restoration album playing on a hidden microphone, symbolizing his thrifty success as he revealed jewels he'd found, a benefit to all, knocking the keel of corruption and whataboutism, the ickiest form of moral evasion, while also allowing a moment for a graceful twerk of triumph; no longer lacking, his membership in the rebuilt community was secure, his handmade work now seen as original, a true display of heroism over the chaos.
panelSentences: A unique dweller in a shacky housing unit, often mocked for his unusual fauvism-inspired handmade art, sometimes borrowed tools; his face showed confusion over whataboutism from critics, lacking formal university education, he nonetheless had insights and faith in his quiet heroism, his kit included a keyring of knives he’d learn to wield, dreaming of restoration for the federations, hoping to transform the abysmal conditions as he lived simply, surrounded by sunflowers grown from wheat, adding vitality to his aged world, often kipping on the floor.

An incident occurred: a twister ripped through, causing a deadlock in the city's hierarchy; the guards were unwilling to help, leaving many hurting and confused as he saw emergents of raccoons scavenging through the ruined housing, the temperature dropped, and a strange mist rose, damping any hope, he knew he had to nominate himself, overcoming his over-reliance on liberalism as a sole solution, he felt a jerk of fear, recognizing the need to conjoin his dormant skills, his brain started working, realizing his primary problem was a lack of icebreaker action.

Suddenly, a cascade of jackhammers began, signaling an uprising from below, causing the ground to waver; the lieutenant, a phantom of the old aristocrat class, watched as the dweller donned a leopard-print backpack, his original plan hatching, using his magic to generate a broad, graceful arc of ice skates from the moisture, he navigated the tangled mysteries of gravity, leaving clear footprints as he moved with gradual, cinematic lighting, avoiding the lustful gaze of the monopoly figures, his brain on stimulants, he managed to juxtapose the chaos with moments of unexpected beauty.

Through the artic air, he voyaged with his electrician's kit, performing upgrades and cleaning, restoring power and light; the drums began to beat, a restoration album playing on a hidden microphone, symbolizing his thrifty success as he revealed jewels he'd found, a benefit to all, knocking the keel of corruption and whataboutism, the ickiest form of moral evasion, while also allowing a moment for a graceful twerk of triumph; no longer lacking, his membership in the rebuilt community was secure, his handmade work now seen as original, a true display of heroism over the chaos.
rawPanelSentences: A unique dweller in a shacky housing unit, often mocked for his unusual fauvism-inspired handmade art, sometimes borrowed tools; his face showed confusion over whataboutism from critics, lacking formal university education, he nonetheless had insights and faith in his quiet heroism, his kit included a keyring of knives he’d learn to wield, dreaming of restoration for the federations, hoping to transform the abysmal conditions as he lived simply, surrounded by sunflowers grown from wheat, adding vitality to his aged world, often kipping on the floor.

An incident occurred: a twister ripped through, causing a deadlock in the city's hierarchy; the guards were unwilling to help, leaving many hurting and confused as he saw emergents of raccoons scavenging through the ruined housing, the temperature dropped, and a strange mist rose, damping any hope, he knew he had to nominate himself, overcoming his over-reliance on liberalism as a sole solution, he felt a jerk of fear, recognizing the need to conjoin his dormant skills, his brain started working, realizing his primary problem was a lack of icebreaker action.

Suddenly, a cascade of jackhammers began, signaling an uprising from below, causing the ground to waver; the lieutenant, a phantom of the old aristocrat class, watched as the dweller donned a leopard-print backpack, his original plan hatching, using his magic to generate a broad, graceful arc of ice skates from the moisture, he navigated the tangled mysteries of gravity, leaving clear footprints as he moved with gradual, cinematic lighting, avoiding the lustful gaze of the monopoly figures, his brain on stimulants, he managed to juxtapose the chaos with moments of unexpected beauty.

Through the artic air, he voyaged with his electrician's kit, performing upgrades and cleaning, restoring power and light; the drums began to beat, a restoration album playing on a hidden microphone, symbolizing his thrifty success as he revealed jewels he'd found, a benefit to all, knocking the keel of corruption and whataboutism, the ickiest form of moral evasion, while also allowing a moment for a graceful twerk of triumph; no longer lacking, his membership in the rebuilt community was secure, his handmade work now seen as original, a true display of heroism over the chaos.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: wearing a patched leopard-print backpack and a single ornate silver keyring. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone figure, the protagonist, sits amidst towering sunflowers in a shacky housing unit, sketching furiously with a small knife, deep in thought, wide-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A swirling twister tears through a futuristic city skyline, causing debris to cascade down onto confused guards, low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, wearing ice skates, gracefully glides over a tangled, glowing pathway, created from ice, avoiding powerful jackhammers below, dynamic action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, now an electrician, high up on a restored power grid, connecting glowing wires, with drums and a microphone subtly visible in the background, close-up on his determined face.

definitions.definition: To receive (something) from somebody temporarily, expecting to return it.
To take money from a bank under the agreement that the bank will be paid over the course of time.
To adopt (an idea) as one's own.
definitions.example: to borrow the style, manner, or opinions of another

definitions.definition: To adopt a word from another language.

In a subtraction, to deduct (one) from a digit of the minuend and add ten to the following digit, in order that the subtraction of a larger digit in the subtrahend from the digit in the minuend to which ten is added gives a positive result.
(Upper Midwestern United States) To lend.
To temporarily obtain (something) for (someone).
To feign or counterfeit.
(obsolete except in ballads) To secure the release of (someone) from prison.
To receive (something) from somebody, with little possibility of returning it.
definitions.example: Can I borrow a sheet of paper?

definitions.definition: A propaganda technique where criticisms are deflected by raising corresponding criticisms of the opposite side.
Any of several cereal grains, of the genus Triticum, that yields flour as used in bakery.
A light brown colour, like that of wheat.
Wheaten, of a light brown colour, like that of wheat.
The qualities characteristic of a hero, such as courage, bravery, fortitude, unselfishness, etc.; the display of such qualities.
An object or other creation (e.g. narrative work) from which all later copies and variations are derived
definitions.example: This manuscript is the original.

definitions.definition: A person with a unique and interesting personality and/or creative talent
definitions.example: You’re a real original.

definitions.definition: An eccentric
Relating to the origin or beginning; preceding all others
definitions.example: the original state of mankind;  the original laws of a country;  the original inventor of a process

definitions.definition: First in a series or copies/versions
definitions.example: The original manuscript contained spelling errors which were fixed in later versions.

definitions.definition: Newly created
definitions.example: Tonight we will hear an original work by one of our best composers.

definitions.definition: Fresh, different
definitions.example: The paper contains a number of original ideas about color perception.

definitions.definition: Pioneering
definitions.example: Parker was one of the original bebop players.

definitions.definition: Having as its origin
definitions.example: This kind of barbecue is original to North Carolina.

definitions.definition: The front part of the head of a human or other animal, featuring the eyes, nose and mouth, and the surrounding area.
definitions.example: That girl has a pretty face.

definitions.definition: One's facial expression.
definitions.example: Why the sad face?

definitions.definition: (in expressions such as 'make a face') A distorted facial expression; an expression of displeasure, insult, etc.
definitions.example: Children! Stop making faces at each other!

definitions.definition: The public image; outward appearance.
definitions.example: He managed to show a bold face despite his embarrassment.

definitions.definition: The frontal aspect of something.
definitions.example: The face of the cliff loomed above them.

definitions.definition: An aspect of the character or nature of someone or something.
definitions.example: Poverty is the ugly face of capitalism.

definitions.definition: Presence; sight; front.
definitions.example: to fly in the face of danger

definitions.definition: The directed force of something.
definitions.example: They turned the boat into the face of the storm.

definitions.definition: Good reputation; standing in the eyes of others; dignity; prestige. (See lose face, save face).
Shameless confidence; boldness; effrontery.
definitions.example: You've got some face coming round here after what you've done.

definitions.definition: Any surface, especially a front or outer one.
definitions.example: Put a big sign on each face of the building that can be seen from the road.

definitions.definition: Any of the flat bounding surfaces of a polyhedron. More generally, any of the bounding pieces of a polytope of any dimension.
The numbered dial of a clock or watch, the clock face.
The mouth.
definitions.example: He's always stuffing his face with chips.

definitions.definition: Makeup; one's complete facial cosmetic application.
definitions.example: I'll be out in a sec. Just let me put on my face.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) A person.
definitions.example: It was just the usual faces at the pub tonight.

definitions.definition: A familiar or well-known person; a member of a particular scene, such as music or fashion scene.
definitions.example: He owned several local businesses and was a face around town.

definitions.definition: A headlining wrestler with a persona embodying heroic or virtuous traits and who is regarded as a "good guy", especially one who is handsome and well-conditioned; a baby face.
definitions.example: The fans cheered on the face as he made his comeback.

definitions.definition: The front surface of a bat.
The part of a golf club that hits the ball.
The side of the card that shows its value (as opposed to the back side, which looks the same on all cards of the deck).
The head of a lion, shown face-on and cut off immediately behind the ears.
The width of a pulley, or the length of a cog from end to end.
definitions.example: a pulley or cog wheel of ten inches face

definitions.definition: A typeface.
Mode of regard, whether favourable or unfavourable; favour or anger.
The amount expressed on a bill, note, bond, etc., without any interest or discount; face value.
(of a person or animal) To position oneself or itself so as to have one's face closest to (something).
definitions.example: Face the sun.

definitions.definition: (of an object) To have its front closest to, or in the direction of (something else).
definitions.example: Turn the chair so it faces the table.

definitions.definition: To cause (something) to turn or present a face or front, as in a particular direction.
To be presented or confronted with; to have in prospect.
definitions.example: We are facing an uncertain future.

definitions.definition: To deal with (a difficult situation or person); to accept (facts, reality, etc.) even when undesirable.
definitions.example: I'm going to have to face this sooner or later.

definitions.definition: To have the front in a certain direction.
definitions.example: The seats in the carriage faced backwards.

definitions.definition: To have as an opponent.
definitions.example: Puddletown United face Mudchester Rovers in the quarter-finals.

definitions.definition: To be the batsman on strike.
definitions.example: Willoughby comes in to bowl, and it's Hobson facing.

definitions.definition: To confront impudently; to bully.
To cover in front, for ornament, protection, etc.; to put a facing upon.
definitions.example: a building faced with marble

definitions.definition: To line near the edge, especially with a different material.
definitions.example: to face the front of a coat, or the bottom of a dress

definitions.definition: To cover with better, or better appearing, material than the mass consists of, for purpose of deception, as the surface of a box of tea, a barrel of sugar, etc.

To make the surface of (anything) flat or smooth; to dress the face of (a stone, a casting, etc.); especially, in turning, to shape or smooth the flat surface of, as distinguished from the cylindrical surface.
(retail) To arrange the products in (a store) so that they are tidy and attractive.
definitions.example: In my first job, I learned how to operate a till and to face the store to high standards.

definitions.definition: A puny or insignificant person, generally male; a twerp.
One who twists.
The instrument used in twisting, or making twists.
A ball delivered with a twist, as in cricket or billiards.
A tornado.
A girder
The inner part of the thigh, the proper place to rest upon when on horseback.
A crook, a villain.
The party game Twister, usually capitalized, or a variant.
The quality of being liberal.
Any political movement founded on the autonomy and personal freedom of the individual, progress and reform, and government by law with the consent of the governed.
An economic ideology in favour of laissez faire and the free market (related to economic liberalism).
A nocturnal omnivore native to North America, typically with a mixture of gray, brown, and black fur, a mask-like marking around the eyes and a striped tail; Procyon lotor.
Any mammal of the genus Procyon.
Any mammal of the subfamily Procyoninae, a procyonine.
Any mammal of the family Procyonidae, a procyonid.
A standstill resulting from the opposition of two evenly matched forces; a stalemate or impasse
An inability to continue due to two programs or devices each requiring a response from the other before completing an operation.
To cause or to come to a deadlock.
A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
Being the only one of its kind; unequaled, unparalleled or unmatched.
definitions.example: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook

definitions.definition: Of a feature, such that only one holder has it.
Particular, characteristic.
Of a rare quality, unusual.
A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.
definitions.example: The prison guard unlocked the door of the cell.   After completing the repairs, he replaced the sump guard.

definitions.definition: A garda; a police officer.
A squad responsible for protecting something.
definitions.example: The president inspected the guard of honour.

definitions.definition: The part of a sword that protects the wielder's hand.
A part of a machine which blocks access to dangerous parts.
definitions.example: The motorcycle mechanic removed the damaged chain guard.

definitions.definition: A watchchain.
A panel of a car that encloses the wheel area, especially the front wheels.
A state of caution; posture of defence.
Something worn to protect part of the body, e.g. the shins in cricket.
A relatively short player, playing farther from the basket than a forward or center.
The position on the popping crease where a batsman makes a mark to align himself with the wicket; see take guard.

Either of two offensive positions between the center and each of the offensive tackles, whose main responsibilities are to protect the quarterback, and open up "holes" through which offensive players can run.
A player playing a position named guard.
An employee, normally travelling in the last vehicle of a train, responsible for the safety of the train.
A Boolean expression that must evaluate to true for a branch of program execution to continue.
To protect from danger; to secure against surprise, attack, or injury; to keep in safety; to defend.
To keep watch over, in order to prevent escape or restrain from acts of violence, or the like.
definitions.example: Guard the prisoner.

definitions.definition: To watch by way of caution or defense; to be caution; to be in a state or position of defense or safety.
definitions.example: Careful people guard against mistakes.

definitions.definition: To protect the edge of, especially with an ornamental border; hence, to face or ornament with lists, laces, etc.
To fasten by binding; to gird.
To be painful.
definitions.example: Does your leg still hurt? / It is starting to feel better.

definitions.definition: To cause (a creature) physical pain and/or injury.
definitions.example: If anybody hurts my little brother, I will get upset.

definitions.definition: To cause (somebody) emotional pain.
To undermine, impede, or damage.
definitions.example: This latest gaffe hurts the legislator’s reelection prospects still further.

definitions.definition: A sensation that hurts.
A ship designed to break through ice so that it, or other ships coming behind, can navigate on frozen seas.
definitions.example: The steel hulls of ice-breakers are much thicker than those of standard vessels.

definitions.definition: A game, activity, humorous anecdote, etc., designed to relax a group of people to help them get to know each other.
definitions.example: The new college hallmates were awkward with each other at first, but after a game of charades as an icebreaker, they were laughing like old friends.

definitions.definition: A lively song and dance routine at the start of a musical.
To mimic, to simulate.
To create an artistic representation of.
To make fun of by mimicking, to taunt.
To tantalise, and disappoint the hopes of.
To create a mockup or prototype of.
definitions.example: What's the best way to mock a database layer?

definitions.definition: The act of learning something
To acquire, or attempt to acquire knowledge or an ability to do something.
To attend a course or other educational activity.
To gain knowledge from a bad experience so as to improve.
definitions.example: learn from one's mistakes

definitions.definition: To study.
definitions.example: I learn medicine.

definitions.definition: To come to know; to become informed of; to find out.
definitions.example: He just learned that he will be sacked.

definitions.definition: An advantage; help or aid from something.
definitions.example: It was for her benefit.   His benefit was free beer.

definitions.definition: A payment made in accordance with an insurance policy or a public assistance scheme.
An event such as a performance, given to raise funds for some cause.
Beneficence; liberality
To be or to provide a benefit to.
To receive a benefit (from); to be a beneficiary.
To cause to grow old; to impart the characteristics of age to.
definitions.example: Grief ages us.

definitions.definition: To postpone an action that would extinguish something, as a debt.
definitions.example: Money's a little tight right now, let's age our bills for a week or so.

definitions.definition: To categorize by age.
definitions.example: One his first assignments was to age the accounts receivable.

definitions.definition: To grow aged; to become old; to show marks of age.
definitions.example: He grew fat as he aged.

definitions.definition: Old people, collectively.
Old.
(chiefly non-US) Having the age of.
definitions.example: Aged 18, he had no idea what to do with his life.

definitions.definition: Having undergone the improving effects of time; matured.
A portable percussive power tool that combines a hammer and chisel used to drill or break hard matter, for instance rock or concrete.
To use a jackhammer.
To break (something) using a jackhammer.
To form (something) using a jackhammer.
To move like a jackhammer.
To move (something) like a jackhammer.
To strike (something) repeatedly with force, to pound.
A large room, especially one used to receive and entertain guests.
A gathering of people for a social or intellectual meeting.
An art gallery or exhibition; especially the Paris salon or autumn salon.
A beauty salon or similar establishment.
The capacity to live and develop.
Energy or vigour.
That which distinguishes living from nonliving things; life, animateness.
A body of authoritative officials organized in nested ranks.
A social, religious, economic or political system or organization in which people or groups of people are ranked with some superior to others based on their status, authority or some other trait.
Any group of objects ranked so that every one but the topmost is subordinate to a specified one above it.
A sight or view of the interior of anything; a deep inspection or view; introspection; frequently used with into.
Power of acute observation and deduction
Knowledge (usually derived from consumer understanding) that a company applies in order to make a product or brand perform better and be more appealing to customers
Intuitive apprehension of the inner nature of a thing or things; intuition.
An extended understanding of a subject resulting from identification of relationships and behaviors within a model, context, or scenario.
An individual's awareness of the nature and severity of one's mental illness.
Panthera pardus, a large wild cat with a spotted coat native to Africa and Asia, especially the male of the species (in contrast to leopardess).
(inexact) A similar-looking, large wild cat named after the leopard.
A lion passant guardant.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Phalanta, having black markings on an orange base.
A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.
A lathe tool for turning down the insides and bottoms of cylinders.
A British gold coin worth 20 shillings, issued by the Commonwealth of England in 1656.
Wide in extent or scope.
definitions.example: the broad expanse of ocean

definitions.definition: Extended, in the sense of diffused; open; clear; full.
Having a large measure of any thing or quality; unlimited; unrestrained.
Comprehensive; liberal; enlarged.
Plain; evident.
definitions.example: a broad hint

definitions.definition: (writing) Unsubtle; obvious.
Free; unrestrained; unconfined.
Gross; coarse; indelicate.
definitions.example: a broad compliment; a broad joke; broad humour

definitions.definition: (of an accent) Strongly regional.
(Gaelic languages) Velarized, i.e. not palatalized.
Pertaining to, or resembling an abyss.
Extremely bad; terrible.
A substance that acts to increase physiological or nervous activity in the body.
Something that promotes activity, interest, or enthusiasm.
An asterism, an arbitrary formation of stars perceived as a figure or pattern, or a division of the sky including it, especially one officially recognised by astronomers.
An image associated with a group of stars.
The configuration of planets at a given time (notably of birth), as used for determining a horoscope.
A wide, seemingly unlimited assortment.
definitions.example: A constellation of possibilities.

definitions.definition: A fleet of satellites of the same purpose (such as the set of GPS satellites, or Iridium satcom fleet).
A configuration or grouping.
A network of connections that exists between people who are in polyamorous relationships, for example between one person, their partner, and that person's partner.
To join together; to unite; to combine.
definitions.example: They are representatives that will loosely conjoin a nation.

definitions.definition: To marry.
definitions.example: I will conjoin you in holy matrimony.

definitions.definition: (grammar) To join as coordinate elements, often with a coordinating conjunction, such as coordinate clauses.
To combine two sets, conditions, or expressions by a logical AND; to intersect.
To unite, to join, to league.
A plant whose root system grows underwater, but whose shoot, leaves and flowers grow up and above the water.
To go on a long journey.
A tradesman who installs, repairs and maintains electrical wiring and equipment.
A scientist who studies electricity.

A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.
A weapon designed with the aforementioned specifications intended for slashing and/or stabbing and too short to be called a sword. A dagger.
Any blade-like part in a tool or a machine designed for cutting, such as that of a chipper.
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.
To entitle, confer a name upon.
definitions.example: 1658: the City of Norwich [...] was enlarged, builded and nominated by the Saxons. — Sir Thomas Browne, Urne-Burial (Penguin 2005, p. 12)

definitions.definition: Nominotypical
definitions.example: the nominate subspecies

definitions.definition: One of the aristocracy, nobility, or people of rank in a community; one of a ruling class; a noble (originally in Revolutionary France).
A proponent of aristocracy; an advocate of aristocratic government.
Something that is unusual; an anomaly.
Not usual, out of the ordinary
definitions.example: Every once in a while, though, Idol comes across an unusual talent who just blows everyone away.

definitions.definition: A set of six legal balls bowled.
Any surplus amount of money, goods delivered, etc.
To go over, or jump over.
definitions.example: He overed the fence in good style.

definitions.definition: To run about.
definitions.example: The cattle have been overing all day because of the flies.

definitions.definition: Discontinued; ended or concluded.
definitions.example: The show is over.

definitions.definition: Thoroughly; completely; from beginning to end.
definitions.example: I'm going to look over our department's expenses.

definitions.definition: To an excessive degree; overly.
From an upright position to being horizontal.
definitions.example: He bent over to touch his toes.

definitions.definition: Horizontally; left to right or right to left.
definitions.example: I moved over to make room for him to sit down.

definitions.definition: From one position or state to another.
definitions.example: Come over and play!

definitions.definition: Overnight (throughout the night).
definitions.example: Can I sleep over?

definitions.definition: (usually with do) Again; another time; once more; over again.
definitions.example: I lost my paper and I had to do the entire assignment over.

definitions.definition: (procedure word) a procedure word meaning that a station is finished transmitting and is expecting a response.
definitions.example: Bravo Six Four, this is Bravo Six Actual. Send your traffic, over.

definitions.definition: Physical positioning.
By comparison.
Indicating relative status, authority, or power
definitions.example: The owner's son lorded over the experienced managers.

definitions.definition: Divided by.
definitions.example: four over two equals two over one

definitions.definition: Separates the three of a kind from the pair in a full house.
definitions.example: 9♦9♠9♣6♥6♠ = nines over sixes

definitions.definition: Finished with; done with; from one state to another via a hindrance that must be solved or defeated; or via a third state that represents a significant difference from the first two.
definitions.example: He is finally over his [distress over the loss of the relationship with his] ex-girlfriend.

definitions.definition: While using, especially while consuming.
Concerning or regarding.
definitions.example: The two boys had a fight over whose girlfriend was the best.

definitions.definition: Above, implying superiority after a contest; in spite of; notwithstanding.
definitions.example: It was a fine victory over their opponents.

definitions.definition: In radio communications: end of sentence, ready to receive reply.
definitions.example: How do you receive? Over!

definitions.definition: Act of joining together into a single political entity.
definitions.example: It is 106 years since federation.

definitions.definition: Array of nations or states that are unified under one central authority which is elected by its members.
Any society or organisation formed from separate groups or bodies.
A collection of network or telecommunication providers that offer interoperability.
To remove dirt from a place or object.
definitions.example: Can you clean the windows today?

definitions.definition: To tidy up, make a place neat.
definitions.example: Clean your room right now!

definitions.definition: To remove equipment from a climbing route after it was previously lead climbed.
To make things clean in general.
definitions.example: She just likes to clean. That’s why I married her.

definitions.definition: To remove unnecessary files, etc. from (a directory, etc.).
To brush the ice lightly in front of a moving rock to remove any debris and ensure a correct line; less vigorous than a sweep.
To purge a raw of any blemishes caused by the scanning process such as brown tinting and poor color contrast.
To remove guts and/or scales of a butchered animal.
(gerund of clean) The process of making something clean.
The afterbirth of cows, ewes, etc.
Water or other liquid finely suspended in air.
definitions.example: It was difficult to see through the morning mist.

definitions.definition: A layer of fine droplets or particles.
definitions.example: There was an oily mist on the lens.

definitions.definition: Anything that dims, darkens, or hinders vision.
To form mist.
definitions.example: It's misting this morning.

definitions.definition: To spray fine droplets on, particularly of water.
definitions.example: I mist my tropical plants every morning.

definitions.definition: To cover with a mist.
definitions.example: The lens was misted.

definitions.definition: (of the eyes) To be covered by tears.
definitions.example: My eyes misted when I remembered what had happened.

definitions.definition: A device (transducer) used to convert sound waves into a varying electric current; normally fed into an amplifier and either recorded or broadcast.
To put one or more microphones on or in.
The process of bringing an object back to its original state; the process of restoring something.
